title: Retryable fail() re-queues with no delay -> immediate retry storm; add backoff options
description:
MongoJobQueue.fail() retry branch (src/backends/MongoJobQueue.ts ~228) sets status back to 'pending' with failReason but leaves runAt unchanged (still <= now) -> job is immediately re-claimable. A handler that throws fast burns all maxAttempts in milliseconds and dead-letters a job a transient failure that would clear seconds later. On a downstream outage, every job of a type fails at once and instant-retries -> CPU/Mongo write storm hammering the dead dependency.

durabl currently has NO per-job retry delay (MIN/MAX_BACKOFF_MS in JobQueue.ts is only poll-loop error backoff).

Fix:
- Add backoff options to EnqueueOptions: backoff ('exponential'|'fixed'|fn), backoffDelay (base ms), backoffMaxDelay (cap ms). Sensible defaults: exponential full jitter, base 1000, cap 60000.
- Apply in fail() retry branch: runAt = now + retryBackoffMs(attempt). Persist backoff config on the job doc at enqueue so fail() can read it.
- Share one retryBackoffMs helper with the reaper (recoveryBackoffMs from du-5ws) instead of two copies.

Regression tests (NON-NEGOTIABLE): fail() retry sets runAt in future (not immediately claimable); exhausted still -> failed; fixed vs exponential honored; custom fn honored.
close_reason: Added backoff to EnqueueOptions (backoff/backoffDelay/backoffMaxDelay), persisted on job doc. fail() retry branch now sets runAt = now + jittered backoff (was: unchanged -> immediate re-claim storm). Shared retryBackoffMs helper (src/backends/backoff.ts) used by both fail() and the reaper; removed duplicate recoveryBackoffMs. Tests: backoff.test.ts (unit) + MongoJobQueue fail() backoff cases. README + types updated.

title: Reaper hot-loop: recoverStuckJobs re-queues without backoff or attempt cap
description:
MongoJobQueue.recoverStuckJobs sets stalled active jobs straight back to status:'pending' with no attempt increment, no backoff, no max-attempt check (src/backends/MongoJobQueue.ts ~line 292).

A handler that wedges the worker stalls -> reclaimed at visibility timeout -> wedges again -> reclaimed... forever, every visibility-timeout window. Hot retry loop.

meteor/jobs PR #14286 (execution.js detectStalledJobs) explicitly routes stalled jobs through the normal failure/retry path BECAUSE immediate re-queue causes this loop. Comment in their code calls it out directly.

Fix: route recovered jobs through retry logic — bump attempts, apply backoff (full jitter), respect maxAttempts; move to 'failed' past the cap instead of looping.

Regression test (NON-NEGOTIABLE per bug-fix rule): wedge a handler so it never heartbeats, run reaper twice across visibility windows, assert attempts increments and job goes to 'failed' at cap rather than cycling pending->active indefinitely.
close_reason: Fixed: recoverStuckJobs now routes stalled jobs through retry/terminal logic — exhausted attempts -> failed, retryable -> pending with jittered backoff runAt (breaks hot retry loop). Regression tests in test/MongoJobQueue.test.ts.

title: Batch heartbeat: single updateMany instead of per-job updateOne
description:
Durabl heartbeats per-job (updateOne x N). At concurrency=N that's N writes per heartbeat cycle.

meteor/jobs PR #14286 (execution.js _batchHeartbeat) does ONE updateMany({_id:{$in:[...runningIds]}, status:'running'}, {$set:{heartbeatAt:now}}) per cycle. N writes -> 1, regardless of concurrency.

Implement a batched heartbeat path in MongoJobQueue/JobQueue: collect all in-flight job ids on this instance, single updateMany per heartbeatInterval. Keep per-job heartbeat() public API for handlers that extend lease explicitly, but the periodic keepalive should batch.

Verify: with 10 concurrent jobs, assert one updateMany call per cycle (spy/mock), not 10 updateOne.
close_reason: Added MongoJobQueue.batchHeartbeat(jobIds): single updateMany over active jobs, N->1 writes per keepalive cycle. Tests cover multi-job extend, completed-job exclusion, empty no-op.

title: Targeted promotion/poll timer for scheduled jobs (fire at soonest runAt)
description:
Durabl polls at fixed interval, so a delayed job (runAt in future) can fire up-to-one-poll-interval late.

meteor/jobs (_scheduleNextPromotion) queries the soonest pending scheduledAt and setTimeout exactly until then (capped at max interval, +50ms buffer), self-adjusting instead of fixed polling. Delayed jobs fire precisely without wasted poll cycles.

Optional enhancement for durabl: when push (change streams) is active and only the 60s safety poll runs, a delayed job could wait up to 60s. Add a targeted timer that wakes at the next runAt. Only worth it if scheduled-job latency matters to consumers. Lower priority.

title: Verify claim concurrency has no TOCTOU under async burst
description:
meteor/jobs (execution.js claimAndExecute) re-checks global concurrency AFTER winning the atomic claim and releases back to ready if over cap — guarding the TOCTOU gap between the 'can I accept?' check and the claim under async bursts.

Durabl: audit JobQueue poll/claim path. claimNext is atomic at the DB, but the processor loop's concurrency cap (per-type / global) is checked in JS before claimNext. Under burst, multiple in-flight claimNext calls can each pass the JS cap check then all win distinct jobs -> over-cap execution on one instance.

Verify whether this can happen; if so, either gate claims through a semaphore that decrements before the await, or release/re-queue post-claim like meteor does. Add a test that fires N concurrent poll ticks against M>cap ready jobs and asserts running never exceeds cap.
close_reason: Confirmed real TOCTOU: claimAndProcess checked activeCount<concurrency then awaited claimNext then incremented; a push burst drove N concurrent claims past the gate, overshooting the per-instance cap (atomic per-job claim does not bound own concurrency). Fix: reserve slot synchronously (pre-increment, rollback if no job/err); poll loop now routes through claimAndProcess removing duplicate raced path. Regression: test/JobQueueConcurrency.test.ts (cap=2 and cap=1 under burst).
